summary: Completed testing of LeadFlow AI basic functionality - health endpoints, auth system, and frontend routing. Database-dependent features fail as expected since DATABASE_URL is not configured.

minor_bugs.issue: Returns 500 instead of 401 when no authentication token provided
minor_bugs.impact: Should return 401 for unauthenticated requests, not database error

expected_failures.issue: Return 500 due to missing DATABASE_URL configuration
expected_failures.impact: Expected behavior - user needs to configure Supabase database

ui_bugs.issue: No error messages shown to user when API calls fail
ui_bugs.impact: Users don't know why login/registration failed

passed_tests: Environment status endpoint (/api/config/env-status) returns correct configuration status
Login page loads correctly with proper branding and styling
Form elements are functional (email, password, full name inputs)
Google OAuth button is present and clickable
Frontend-backend API integration works (calls are made successfully)
Root path (/) redirects to /Login when unauthenticated
Protected routes redirect to /Login when unauthenticated
Form submissions trigger API calls to backend
action_item_for_main_agent: 1. User must provide DATABASE_URL (Supabase Transaction Pooler URI) to enable auth functionality. 2. Consider adding user-friendly error messages for failed login/registration attempts. 3. Minor fix: /api/auth/me should return 401 instead of 500 for unauthenticated requests.
